title: The page does not calculate
checkLabels.t7: The sum is in the code
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted everything instead of adding a line. Put the code back.
why.untouched: script.js is still empty. That is where you write in this lesson.
hint2.untouched: Open the script.js tab. The other two files stay as they are.
why.not-closed: A tag in index.html is open and never closed.
hint2.not-closed: index.html is not part of this lesson. If you edited it, put it back the way it was.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page. The file exists, but nothing calls it.
hint2.not-linked: The last line in index.html, right before the end of body, links script.js. Check that it is there and that the name matches letter for letter.
hint3.not-linked: If the file is called script.js and the line asks for scripts.js, the browser finds nothing and says nothing. One letter is enough.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error before it reached the end.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below. The red line says what it did not like, and where.
hint3.crashed: The most common one on day one is a missing bracket or quote. A line that starts with console.log(42 and never closes the bracket stops everything after it.
why.nothing-written: script.js is empty. There is nothing to run.
why.frozen: Your code never stops. It loops forever and was cut off.
hint2.frozen: One line is enough for this lesson. If you wrote a loop, remove it. You do not need it yet.
why.no-output: The code ran, but nothing came out. The console is empty.
hint2.no-output: console.log is the part that shows. Without it the sum is calculated and thrown away.
hint3.no-output: Write only 12 + 30 and the computer works out 42, then forgets it immediately. Nobody asked it to show anything.
why.wrong-number: A number came out, but not this one. The total is different.
hint2.wrong-number: The multiplication sign in JavaScript is not x. The letter x is a name, not an action.
hint4.wrong-number: Do the same with the two numbers from the page — how many coffees, and at what price.
why.typed-answer: The number is right, but you typed it by hand. The computer calculated nothing. It only repeated something you already knew.
hint2.typed-answer: The point of the lesson is that the sum lives in the code. Leave the two numbers and the sign between them, not the result.
why.lost-p: A line has disappeared from the page. index.html is not part of this lesson.
blocks.0.text: Look at the page on the right. Coffees sold: 1247. Price: 3. Total: 1247 x 3. That is not a sum. Those are characters. The page shows exactly what you typed into it, letter for letter.
blocks.1.text: HTML repeats. It does not calculate. There is no tag for multiply, no tag for remember, no tag for if. CSS will not help either — it decides how the text looks, not what it says.
blocks.3.text: JavaScript performs actions. It does not describe the page, it does things to it. It lives in a separate file, script.js, and runs from top to bottom, line by line, the moment the page opens.
blocks.5.text: console.log shows. Inside the brackets you put whatever you want to see. Here it is 12 + 30 — but 12 + 30 is not what appears. 42 appears. The sum happens before the showing.
blocks.7.text: Not on the page. Open the Console panel below. The preview and the console are two different places: the preview is what a visitor sees, the console is what you see while you write.
blocks.8.text: That is why the console comes first in this course. From here on, every error goes through it.
blocks.9.text: The computer does not guess. It does not work anything out in its head and does nothing you have not told it explicitly. That sounds like a flaw. It is the only reason code is predictable.
blocks.11.text: Open script.js. Print the total: 1247 coffees at 3 each. The number should appear in the console.
blocks.12.text: Do not work it out in your head and type the answer. The sum has to be in the code — that is the only thing that matters in this lesson, and the checks look for it.
